what_it_does: An HTML/CSS desktop mockup for the A11 'Portals — live transclusion' feature, showing a canvas with a source process node, multiple portal 'live window' frames, an inspector panel, a left palette rail, and inline annotations that distinguish existing backend behavior from proposed visual treatment.
observations: The portal node is treated as a content-kind node, not a computed/process node.
Portal status is shown as 'live' or 'empty' rather than the usual ran/cached/stuck dots.
A dangling portal reference renders an honest empty state with output None, not fabricated content.
SVG link lines connect the source node to each live portal to visualize the transclusion relationship.
An identity tag claims the source and portals share the same content-hash ('one artefact, many views').
The mockup registers a dedicated ui://canvas/portal-window address for portal window frames.
The inspector panel exposes config.ref, RESOLVE=reference, and notes that the scheduler skips the node.
The left rail highlights 'portal' in the content palette with a ⊕ chip.
A legend explains the portal-specific vocabulary (live window, live, empty) separately from computed-node status.
The HTML header comment extensively grounds the mockup in real code and test files.
standouts: The deliberate, honest portal status vocabulary (live / empty) kept separate from computed-node ran/cached/stuck.
The heavy grounding annotation that maps every represented concept to real code/tests and addresses.
The 'one artefact, many views' identity claim tied to identical content-hash.
The proposed translucent 'live window' visual treatment distinct from normal content nodes.
conventions: Uses the shared design-system.css variables and grid layout areas (top, rail, canvas, foot, panel).
Uses data-ui-ref attributes and ui:// address conventions throughout.
Uses mono-chip and kind-color conventions for node types.
Breaks from generic node rendering by proposing dashed, glassy portal frames and source-to-portal link lines.
Includes long explanatory comments inside the HTML rather than keeping design rationale external.
concerns: SVG link lines use hardcoded coordinates, so they are illustrative only and not responsive.
Multiple portal-window elements carry the same ui://canvas/portal-window address, which may blur instance identity.
The mockup repeats the same placeholder body text in every portal, making live-identity hard to verify visually.
No script or interaction is present; it is a static probe, not a working view.
Some proposed styles are scoped locally rather than living in the shared design-system.css.
notes: A grounded design probe that tries to give portal nodes their own honest visual language while explicitly labeling which parts already exist in code and which are still proposals.
questions: Does ui://canvas/portal-window identify a component type or each individual portal window instance?
How would the source-to-portal link line be rendered dynamically as nodes move?
Should the empty portal offer an affordance to create or repair the missing ref?
Will the proposed portal styles move into design-system.css or remain scoped to this view?
Does the live badge update on every state() call, or only when the canvas redraws?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file is explicitly labeled as a draft/probe mockup for A11 portals and presents both existing backend behavior and a proposed UI treatment.
intention_signals: To design a visual language for portal nodes that makes them read as live windows onto a source address, not computed copies, while keeping the UI honest about scheduler behavior and grounding every claim in real code.
novelty: The explicit separation of portal status from the standard node status dots, combined with an annotation layer that marks every element as either EXISTING or PROPOSED and ties it to specific code and test files.
contribution: A proposed portal visual treatment and a grounded annotation pattern that maps UI concepts to real code, tests, and registered addresses, which a fused system could keep as design intent and traceability.
summary_for_embedding: A grounded HTML/CSS desktop mockup for the A11 portal live-transclusion feature showing source nodes, live portal windows, an inspector, and explicit existing-vs-proposed annotations.
intention_for_embedding: To visualize how portal nodes should appear as honest, live, reference-resolved windows onto a single source artefact rather than as computed or copied content.
